message:
fix(connector): per-decision ordering verification, duplicate message rejection, and bounded entry limit

control_evidence.rs — three correctness fixes:
1. verify_ordering() now tracks ordering per decision_id using a
   BTreeMap instead of a single global last_key. Previously, interleaved
   entries for different decision IDs would incorrectly trigger ordering
   violations even though each decision's entries were individually
   ordered. The new approach only flags violations when entries within
   the SAME decision_id go backwards in (timestamp_ms, chosen_action).
2. Fixes push_bounded() call to use MAX_ENTRIES (4096) instead of
   MAX_EVENTS for the entries vector, matching the intended capacity
   constant. MAX_ENTRIES was previously declared but unused (had
   #[allow(dead_code)]).
3. Updates existing ordering violation tests to use same-decision_id
   entries (which is the scenario that should actually trigger
   violations) and adds test_interleaved_decision_ids_do_not_trigger_
   ordering_violation to confirm that different decision IDs interleaved
   by timestamp are now correctly accepted.

retention_policy.rs — duplicate message ID rejection:
1. Adds DuplicateMessageId variant to RetentionError with error code
   CPR_DUPLICATE_MESSAGE_ID.
2. Inserts an early-return check in RetentionStore::store() that rejects
   messages whose message_id already exists in the store, preventing
   silent storage byte corruption from double-counting.
3. Adds duplicate_message_id_rejected_without_corrupting_storage_bytes
   test verifying that the duplicate is rejected, total_bytes remains
   unchanged, and the decision audit trail contains exactly one entry.
4. Extends the error code coverage test to include the new variant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
